content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN integers, and an integer KKK (1≤K≤N1 \leq K \leq N1≤K≤N).

Find the number of subarrays of AAA with length KKK whose bitwise OR is odd.

**Note:** A subarray of AAA is a contiguous segment of elements of AAA.

For example, if A=[1,3,2]A = [1, 3, 2]A=[1,3,2], then it has 666 non-empty subarrays: [1],[3],[2],[1,3],[3,2],[1,3,2][1], [3], [2], [1, 3], [3, 2], [1, 3, 2][1],[3],[2],[1,3],[3,2],[1,3,2].

In particular, [1,2][1, 2][1,2] is not a subarray of AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array and the subarray size you have to check, respectively.
The second line of each test contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of length-KKK subarrays of AAA whose bitwise OR is odd.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests doesn't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are four subarrays of length K=2K = 2K=2.

[5,7][5, 7][5,7], with bitwise OR equal to 777.
[7,13][7, 13][7,13], with bitwise OR equal to 151515.
[13,4][13, 4][13,4], with bitwise OR equal to 131313.
[4,6][4, 6][4,6], with bitwise OR equal to 666.

Three of them are odd, so the answer is 333.
Test case 222: There are two subarrays of length three, both of them have odd bitwise OR.
